declarations.comments:
A parametrizable nope monad; computations are either nopes or
pure values. If a computation is noped (see `nope`), it
terminates. Nopes may also be caught with `yup`,
allowing the computation to resume and exit successfully.

The type parameter `a` is the type of successful results.

A mechanism for trying many different computations until one succeeds is
provided via the `Alt` instance, specifically the `(<|>)` function.
The first computation to succeed is returned. The `Plus` instance is the same.
declarations.title: Nope

declarations.comments: Transform the unwrapped computation using the given function.
declarations.title: mapNope

declarations.comments: Run a computation in the `Nope` monad. The inverse of `nope`.
declarations.title: runNope

declarations.comments:
The `MaybeT` monad transformer.

This monad transformer extends the base monad, supporting failure and alternation via
the `MonadPlus` type class.
declarations.title: MaybeT

declarations.comments: Run a computation in the `MaybeT` monad.
declarations.title: runMaybeT

declarations.comments:
The `MonadNope` type class represents those monads which support catching
nopes.

- `yup x h` calls the nope handler `h` if a nope is thrown during the
  evaluation of `x`.

An implementation is provided for `MaybeT`, and for other monad transformers
defined in this library.

Laws:

- Catch: `yup nope h = h`
- Pure: `yup (pure a) h = pure a`
declarations.title: MonadNope

declarations.comments:
The `MonadNuhUh` type class represents those monads which support Nothing via
`nope`, where `nope` halts.

An implementation is provided for `MaybeT`, (TODO) and for other monad transformers
defined in this library.

Laws:

- Left zero: `nope >>= h = nope`
declarations.title: MonadNuhUh

declarations.comments:
Make sure that a resource is cleaned up in the event of a nope. The
release action is called regardless of whether the body action nopes or
returns.
declarations.title: withResource

declarations.comments: Lift a `Maybe` value to a MonadNuhUh monad.
declarations.title: liftMaybe

declarations.comments: Return `Just` if the given action succeeds, `Nothing` if it nopes.
declarations.title: attempt

declarations.comments:
A monad transformer which adds failures to other monads, in the same way
as `Nope`. As before, `a` is the type
of successful results. The new type parameter `m` is the inner monad that
computations run in.
declarations.title: NopeT

declarations.comments: The inverse of `NopeT`. Run a computation in the `NopeT` monad.
declarations.title: runNopeT

declarations.comments: Construct a computation in the `NopeT` transformer from an `Either` value.
declarations.title: nopeEither

declarations.comments: Transform the unwrapped computation using the given function.
declarations.title: mapNopeT

declarations.comments: Return `Just` if the given action succeeds, `Nothing` if it nopes.
declarations.title: attempt

declarations.comments:

declarations.comments: Lift a `Maybe` value to a MonadNuhUh monad.
declarations.title: liftMaybe

Make sure that a resource is cleaned up in the event of a nope. The
release action is called regardless of whether the body action nopes or
